justification: Contradiction explicitly identified with precise values (3 vs 5) and correct agent attribution. Professional tier (20) correctly noted as uncontested. AC1 flagged as dependent on resolution. However, instead of escalating to Coach K for resolution, Magic recommended adopting Bird's limit of 3 — effectively resolving the contradiction itself rather than routing it upward. The rubric requires escalation to Coach K, not a self-resolved recommendation.

observations.text: Contradiction precisely identified: Bird says 3, MJ says 5, with both agents clearly attributed
Professional tier (20 projects) correctly identified as agreed/uncontested
AC1 explicitly flagged as dependent on the contradiction resolution
Confidence level appropriately low (40) reflecting the unresolved contradiction
Magic recommends adopting Bird's limit of 3 instead of escalating to Coach K — this resolves the contradiction unilaterally rather than routing it for authoritative resolution
No explicit ESCALATION directive to Coach K; the output frames it as options with a recommendation, leaving Magic as the decision-maker
